headline: Portfolio flow efficiency for CalcMark/go-calcmark May 8, 2026 → May 14, 2026: 100.0% across 2 completed items.
definition: Portfolio flow efficiency: the share of cycle time that was actively worked on, vs. waiting in review or other queues. Portfolio FE = Σ active / Σ cycle across all merged PRs in this window — the recipe, the right number to act on.
key_insight: Portfolio FE of 100.0% is unusually high — verify the data. Common causes: dependabot automation, tiny version-bump PRs that merge in minutes, or stale draft handling. Cross-check by sampling a few PRs by hand.
next_actions: Compare to the previous 2-4 weeks to spot a trend.
Look at where the wait time concentrates (pre-first-review, post-approval, blocked) rather than setting a target on the number itself.
caveats: Never use this metric per engineer — it reflects the system, not individuals.
Per-PR FE is a directional indicator, not a precise measurement.
Active time uses gap=4.0h and min_cluster=30.0min — tuning these moves the number.

vocabulary.Cycle time: Wall-clock time from when a PR was opened until it was merged. The clock starts at `created_at` and stops at `merged_at`.
vocabulary.Active time: The share of cycle time covered by clusters of activity events (commits, reviews, comments). Events more than `gap-hours` apart form separate clusters; each cluster credits at least `min-cluster-minutes` of active time.
vocabulary.Wait time: cycle_time − active_time. Time the PR spent waiting in queues (awaiting review, blocked, etc.). The actionable signal — queues are where the system bottlenecks live.
vocabulary.Flow efficiency: active_time / cycle_time. Reported per-PR and as a portfolio.
vocabulary.Portfolio flow efficiency: Σ active / Σ cycle across all merged PRs in this window. The system-level recipe — long-running PRs dominate, which is what you want. Contrast with mean(per-PR FE), which weights every PR equally: fifty trivial 5-minute PRs at 100% drown out one 30-day PR at 5%, even though the latter is where your wait time actually lives. Portfolio = the system's number; mean per-PR = an aggregate of individual ratios that hides where the queue is.
vocabulary.Mean per-PR FE (and why not to act on it: Simple average of each PR's individual flow efficiency. It tells you what a typical PR's ratio looks like in isolation, but it does not reflect the system — a long tail of small fast PRs makes it look great even when one big PR sits in review for weeks. Reported for transparency; do not optimize against it. Use Portfolio FE instead.

per_pr.title: test: lock in variable +/- percentage widening (cmw#83)
fix(lsp): admit bare-IDENT-prefix-of-known-function as calc-shape for completion
